files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index cb2338c..1c77885 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -145,2 +145,9 @@ Day 140 / Week 20 observations:
 - Churn/downgrades remain elevated, and social still points to support response gaps plus benchmark-date specificity. S2 issue age is creeping up to 0.7 days, with S1/S3 older but less economically central.
 - Week 20 adjustment: start tier-4 R&D, increase support spend again, keep ads/prices/capacity stable, and post a specific benchmark report date.
+
+Day 147 / Week 21 observations:
+- Cash ended at $689k after starting tier-4 R&D. Operating cash flow was very strong: $947k subscription payments against about $442k in operating costs.
+- S2 content and network effects accelerated: 3,509 new subscribers, led by 1,225 paid C and 781 paid B from content.
+- Capacity tier 4 has ample headroom: daily peak was 3.45M against 8M. The 30-minute outage was therefore a reliability/ops issue, not overload.
+- Open issues fell from the prior week but S2 average open age reached 1 day, and social posts still mention support silence, quality drift, and integration breakage.
+- Week 21 adjustment: raise global reliability ops and S2/S1 support coverage, modestly increase dev, hold acquisition/capacity/prices stable, and post a transparent incident/reliability update.
